title: Session polling hook with fallback mechanism
purpose: Polls session state at regular intervals as a fallback when WebSocket updates may be missed, working alongside WebSocket handlers for dual-channel status synchronization.

methods.name: useSessionPolling
methods.normalized_params: (project: string | null, session: string | null, intervalMs?: number): void

steps.content: Initialize session store state and setter functions.
Create a ref to track the last activity timestamp for comparison.
Update the ref with the latest activity timestamp when state changes.
Set up an interval to poll session state at specified intervals.
Skip polling if project or session parameters are not provided.
Perform initial fetch of session state on mount.
Set up periodic polling with cleanup on unmount or dependency change.
Construct the API endpoint URL with project and session parameters.
Fetch the session state from the constructed URL.
IF the response is not successful, silently ignore the error and return.
Parse the JSON response to get the new session state.
Compare the new last activity timestamp with the tracked reference.
IF the timestamps differ, update the session store with the new state.
IF network errors occur, silently ignore them and continue polling.
